message:
fix(database): correct MySQL auth nonce parsing and PostgreSQL error handling robustness

MySQL: Fix authentication handshake nonce parsing that incorrectly used
take_while(|&b| b != 0) to strip trailing nulls from the auth plugin data.
This would also strip valid embedded 0x00 bytes within the nonce, causing
authentication failures with servers whose nonces happen to contain zero bytes.
Now only strips the single trailing null byte if present.

PostgreSQL: Replace fragile unwrap_err() calls in error response handling with
match expressions that handle both Ok and Err variants. The old code assumed
parse_error_response() always returns Err, which would cause a panic if it ever
returned Ok. This fix appears in both the query execution and prepared statement
execution paths, making the error extraction robust regardless of variant.

Both files also include the HashMap → BTreeMap migration for column indices and
connection parameters, consistent with the codebase-wide deterministic iteration
refactor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
